messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Introduction to Statistics in R course - online, 19-22 Jan
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Introduction to Statistics in R course - online, 19-22 Jan
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

We are pleased to announce that only 2 seats remain for the upcoming online course ?Introduction to Statistics in R? taking place from 19 to 22 January.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ )
 
This beginner-friendly course is designed for early-career researchers and practitioners eager to learn statistical analysis using R. It covers data manipulation, descriptive and inferential statistics, linear and generalized linear models, and more, with a focus on hands-on learning.
Secure your spot now to enhance your data analysis skills with R!
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Time Series Analysis and Forecasting in R course
messages.subject_clean: [R-sig-dyn-mod] Time Series Analysis and Forecasting in R course
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,
I would like to share information about an upcoming online training course that may be of interest to members of the r-sig-dynamic-models community: Time Series Analysis and Forecasting in R
 
Dates: 20?24 July

 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/time-series-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/time-series-in-r/ ) 
 
This 5-day course focuses on advanced time series analysis and forecasting techniques. Unlike many traditional courses that emphasize classical models such as ARIMA or Exponential Smoothing, this course introduces powerful Bayesian dynamic models implemented in Stan via R packages like mvgam and brms. These tools allow modeling complex ecological time series with features such as overdispersion, clustering, missingness, discreteness, and nonlinear effects.
 
Topics covered include data wrangling and visualization, dynamic generalized linear and additive models (GLMs and GAMs), temporal autocorrelation, multivariate time series, and probabilistic forecasting and evaluation.
 
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )
